title: Zyplo - Team Workspace & Resource Management Platform
description: A collaborative workspace platform designed to help teams create and manage workspaces, organize resources, maintain structured documentation, and monitor activity through a unified dashboard. Zyplo focuses on improving team collaboration by centralizing workflows, resources, and communication in one platform.
problem: Teams often rely on scattered tools for communication, documentation, and resource management, resulting in poor organization, lack of visibility, and slow decision-making. Zyplo addresses this by centralizing workspace activities, structured resources, and team insights.
decisions: Designed a workspace-based architecture allowing users to create, manage, and delete multiple workspaces with role-based access control.
Implemented JWT-based authentication to secure APIs and manage user sessions.
Used dynamic routing to manage workspace pages, resource details, and member access.
Structured resource and documentation modules to allow categorized and scalable content management.
Built analytics dashboards using Recharts to visualize workspace activity, resource usage, and team engagement.
Adopted reusable UI components and modular architecture to ensure scalability and maintainability.
features: Authentication system with secure login and registration.
Role-based access control for members and admins.
Interactive dashboards with charts and statistics.
Responsive UI optimized for desktop and mobile.
Dynamic routing for workspace and resource pages.
highlights: Developed the complete workspace management flow including creation, editing, and deletion.
Implemented a structured resource and documentation system with category-based organization.
Built an overview dashboard displaying charts, stats, and activity insights.
Created reusable components for cards, forms, tables, and dashboards.
Ensured clean UI/UX with animations and smooth navigation.
outcomes: Improved team collaboration by centralizing workflows, resources, and insights.
Enhanced visibility into workspace activity through analytics dashboards.
Provided a scalable foundation for adding future collaboration tools and integrations.

title: VELANO Fashion - Premium Fashion Ecommerce Platform
description: A full-stack fashion ecommerce platform for a Bangladesh-based premium clothing brand, combining a polished storefront, integrated admin operations, localized checkout, and membership-driven customer retention flows.
problem: Fashion brands need more than a basic storefront. They need a branded buying experience, flexible catalog management, localized payment and shipping support, and systems that encourage repeat purchases.
decisions: Structured the project as separate Next.js frontend and Express TypeScript backend applications to keep storefront and API responsibilities clear.
Implemented JWT-based authentication with profile management, Google sign-in, and role-based admin access.
Built a rich product model supporting sizes, colors, media galleries, reviews, and related products for better merchandising.
Added localized checkout support for Bangladesh with bKash, Nagad, and cash-on-delivery payment flows.
Introduced a membership and rewards system with spend-based qualification logic to improve retention.
Used an asynchronous email notification pipeline so customer and admin order events remain reliable and recoverable.
highlights: Developed both the customer storefront and integrated admin dashboard in a single product ecosystem.
Built guest and authenticated checkout flows with order tracking, payment verification, and profile-based order history.
Implemented wishlist, review, and membership experiences to support repeat customer engagement.
Created admin workflows for managing products, users, orders, and commerce-related operations.
Added SEO metadata, structured site architecture, and branded UI patterns for a premium ecommerce feel.
outcomes: Delivered a stronger direct-to-customer commerce foundation than a standard template storefront.
Improved operational visibility through admin tools, order management, and notification workflows.
Created a scalable brand platform ready for future growth in catalog, loyalty, and marketing features.

title: AssetVerse - HR and Asset Management Platform
description: A full-stack platform for companies to manage employees, assets, requests, role-based access, analytics dashboards, and subscription packages.
problem: Small companies often track assets and HR requests with spreadsheets and disconnected tools, causing slow approvals and weak visibility.
decisions: Implemented role-based route protection and JWT-backed APIs for secure access by user type.
Used TanStack Query for server-state management and responsive data refresh.
Integrated Stripe for package-based subscription flows.
highlights: Built employee and asset lifecycle modules with request and approval flows.
Added dashboard analytics and status tracking for faster decision making.
Designed a responsive UI to support desktop and mobile workflows.
outcomes: Reduced friction in request handling by centralizing key operations in one platform.
Improved visibility with real-time status views and dashboard metrics.
Shipped a scalable foundation ready for additional business modules.

title: ClubNest - Community Club and Event Platform
description: A community web app where users can discover, join, create, and manage clubs and local events.
problem: Communities need a single platform to organize events, manage memberships, and improve participation.
decisions: Designed modular event and club workflows to keep user actions simple.
Used Firebase auth for secure user onboarding and role-aware features.
Applied motion and visual hierarchy to improve discoverability and engagement.
highlights: Implemented event creation, joining, and management experiences.
Built searchable and browsable club/event listings.
Added responsive interactions to keep the platform usable across devices.
outcomes: Created a practical community workflow from discovery to participation.
Improved clarity of event ownership and membership actions.
Delivered a polished, production-style UX with strong frontend execution.

title: Cleanliness and Issue Reporting Portal
description: A civic reporting system where users submit local issues, track complaint status, and communicate with authorities.
problem: Citizens often cannot report and track local service issues in a transparent way.
decisions: Structured complaint lifecycle states to make progress visible to users.
Prioritized simple reporting forms to increase successful submissions.
Created status-oriented UI to reduce uncertainty after reporting.
highlights: Built complaint submission, updates, and tracking flows.
Enabled authority communication context through structured issue data.
Focused on responsive and accessible reporting screens.
outcomes: Made issue reporting easier and more transparent.
Improved trust by exposing clear complaint progress states.
Delivered a reusable civic-reporting architecture.

title: Local Food Lovers Network
description: A community-driven platform where users discover local food spots, write reviews, and connect with nearby food enthusiasts.
problem: Food discovery is fragmented, and local recommendations are hard to trust or search effectively.
decisions: Designed list-and-review flows for quick discovery and contribution.
Used structured category and location metadata to improve browsing.
Kept interface lightweight and mobile-friendly for daily use.
highlights: Implemented food spot exploration and user review experiences.
Added social-style interaction patterns for community engagement.
Built clean UI components for scalable feature additions.
outcomes: Improved discoverability of local food options through shared reviews.
Enabled repeat engagement with a community-centered flow.
Delivered a reusable social discovery foundation.

title: Ember & Oak - Premium Restaurant Demo Website
description: A high-conversion restaurant web experience designed for a premium fine-dining brand, with cinematic motion, rich storytelling, menu exploration, reservation flows, and hospitality-focused conversion paths.
problem: High-end hospitality brands need more than a simple landing page. They need a design-forward digital experience that builds trust, showcases atmosphere, and drives reservation intent.
decisions: Used the Next.js App Router with modular route-level pages to structure the restaurant journey from discovery to reservation.
Built reusable UI sections and layout components to keep the experience scalable across menu, gift card, private dining, and press pages.
Applied motion deliberately with cinematic transitions, progressive reveals, and layered overlays to reinforce a luxury brand feel.
Focused on mobile and desktop conversion by adding persistent reservation affordances and sticky booking UI patterns.
Used typed mock data and component composition to keep content-driven sections maintainable and easy to extend.
highlights: Designed a complete restaurant funnel including menu browsing, reservations, private dining, gift cards, and press coverage.
Built polished interactions such as gallery lightboxes, modals, mobile drawer navigation, and animated section reveals.
Created a strong visual system with luxury typography, dark-surface styling, and high-end hospitality presentation.
Implemented responsive layouts that preserve both visual impact and usability across devices.
outcomes: Delivered a strong sales-demo experience for hospitality-focused web presentation.
Showcased frontend craftsmanship through branded motion, component reuse, and conversion-oriented page architecture.
Created a flexible foundation that could be extended into a real restaurant marketing site or booking product.

title: Study Abroad Counselor - Interactive University Recommendation Experience
description: An interactive study abroad guidance experience that walks students through a multilingual consultation flow, analyzes their preferences, and presents personalized university recommendations in a polished, assistant-style interface.
problem: Students exploring international education often feel overwhelmed by country, degree, budget, language, and career decisions, making it hard to identify realistic and high-fit options.
decisions: Designed the experience as a guided multi-step questionnaire to simplify decision-making and reduce cognitive overload.
Added bilingual Bangla and English support to make the product more accessible to local students.
Used a structured recommendation engine to map user answers into curated university shortlists with contextual reasoning.
Persisted questionnaire and result state across screens to keep the experience smooth and conversational.
Applied motion, assistant-style interactions, and progressive analysis states to make the guidance flow feel more engaging.
highlights: Built an onboarding flow that collects destination, degree, budget, subject, academic background, English proficiency, and career goals.
Created personalized university recommendation cards with match scores, program suggestions, and scholarship context.
Implemented multilingual interface behavior and a polished counselor-style user journey.
Focused on frontend storytelling and product experience rather than a generic form-based workflow.
outcomes: Made a complex study abroad discovery process feel more guided and user-friendly.
Demonstrated strong frontend product thinking through structured flows and personalized results.
Created a reusable foundation for future expansion into a richer counseling or advisory platform.
